surface.name: Pulumi Cloud + AWS get started: first pulumi up
surface.selection_basis: The official documentation presents this as the hosted path from account setup to Deploy with pulumi up. It reaches the requested first-success boundary without extending into post-success production work.

entry_point.developer_intent: Pulumi creates the getting-started AWS resource and records a successful stack update in Pulumi Cloud.
assumed_prior_state: The developer has no prior Pulumi setup for this journey.
The journey starts at account creation and stops at the first documented successful outcome.

documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Pulumi creates the getting-started AWS resource and records a successful stack update in Pulumi Cloud.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: pulumi up completes successfully and reports the created resource and stack outputs.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The official documentation leads the developer to: Deploy with pulumi up.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the first observable happy-path result on the selected Pulumi Cloud + AWS get started: first pulumi up surface; later configuration and production hardening occur after success.

prerequisites.requirement: An AWS account with credentials authorized to create the tutorial resource.
The Pulumi CLI, an AWS credential configuration, and a supported project runtime.

primary_path.action: Create or sign in to Pulumi Cloud.
primary_path.input: A new developer starting at account creation
primary_path.output: The Pulumi Cloud organization is accessible.
primary_path.success_signal: The Pulumi Cloud organization is accessible.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Install and authenticate the Pulumi CLI, then configure AWS credentials.
primary_path.output: The CLI can access Pulumi Cloud and AWS.
primary_path.success_signal: The CLI can access Pulumi Cloud and AWS.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Create the AWS getting-started project and review its generated resource code.
primary_path.output: A Pulumi stack and starter project exist.
primary_path.success_signal: A Pulumi stack and starter project exist.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Run pulumi up and approve the proposed deployment.
primary_path.output: The stack update creates the AWS resource successfully.
primary_path.success_signal: The stack update creates the AWS resource successfully.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

primary_path.action: Inspect the completed update and its reported resources or outputs.
primary_path.output: pulumi up reports a successful update.
primary_path.success_signal: pulumi up reports a successful update.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The path cannot continue until this documented requirement succeeds.

friction_gates.description: AWS credentials need permission to create the resource in the selected region.
AWS resource usage can incur charges under the AWS account.

sources.title: Deploy with pulumi up
evidence_supported: Pulumi creates the getting-started AWS resource and records a successful stack update in Pulumi Cloud.
pulumi up completes successfully and reports the created resource and stack outputs.

uncertainties.question: Does the current fresh-account UI exactly match every navigation label in the documented Deploy with pulumi up path?
uncertainties.impact: Navigation wording may differ while the required outcome and boundary remain the same.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official documentation establishes the journey but account-specific UI state can vary.
